the actor uses a fraudulent, but legitimately created LinkedIn proWle to initiate contact with individuals at the targeted company by sending invitations with a short message. This appears as a benign email Adversaries may gather information about the victim's identity that can be used during targeting. often suggests the recipient click on a link Adversaries may send spearphishing emails with a malicious link in an attempt to gain access to victim systems.

this actor used an attached PDF with embedded URLs or other malicious attachments. Adversaries may send spearphishing emails with a malicious attachment in an attempt to gain access to victim systems.

The landing page initiates a download of a Microsoft Word Wle with malicious macros Tools or files may be copied from an external adversary-controlled system to the victim network through the command and control channel.

If the recipient enables macros, the "More_eggs" payload will be downloaded and executed. An adversary may rely upon a user opening a malicious file in order to gain execution. Users may be subjected to social engineering to get them to open a file that will lead to code execution.

the landing page may initiate the download of a JScript loader instead, but this intermediate malware still ultimately results in the delivery of More_eggs. Adversaries may abuse various implementations of JavaScript for execution.
